children.text: Procurement of citizenship or naturalization unlawfully
Whoever knowingly procures or attempts to procure, contrary to law, the naturalization of any person, or documentary or other evidence of naturalization or of citizenship; or

Whoever, whether for himself or another person not entitled thereto, knowingly issues, procures or obtains or applies for or otherwise attempts to procure or obtain naturalization, or citizenship, or a declaration of intention to become a citizen, or a certificate of arrival or any certificate or evidence of nationalization or citizenship, documentary or otherwise, or duplicates or copies of any of the foregoing—
Shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 25 years (if the offense was committed to facilitate an act of international terrorism (as defined in

children.tail: )), 20 years (if the offense was committed to facilitate a drug trafficking crime (as defined in

)), 10 years (in the case of the first or second such offense, if the offense was not committed to facilitate such an act of international terrorism or a drug trafficking crime), or 15 years (in the case of any other offense), or both.

children.text: Pub. L. 104–208, div. C, title II, § 211(a)(2)
Pub. L. 107–273, div. B, title IV, § 4002(a)(3)
Based on subsections (a) pars. (2)–(5), (7), (b), and (d) of
Section consolidates five similar paragraphs, and the punishment provisions of subsection (d) of said

children.tail: , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Aliens and Nationality, with minor necessary changes in translations and phraseology. Numerous references to aiding and assisting were omitted as unnecessary as such persons are principals under definitive

children.text: Words “a certificate of arrival or” were inserted before “any certificate” in subsection (b), so as to remove any doubt as to scope of section.
